programmes.show_description: How do they make giant floating power stations that provide power around the world? Plus, how do they fabricate the world's smallest aircraft?
How do they create coloured crayons used by children and artists around the world? Plus, how do artisans and craftsmen create treasured mirrors in India?
BMW Isetta. Mike tracks down an Isetta Bubble. Part-car and part-motorbike, this classic is great to drive, but will be expensive to restore. Can Edd and Mike make a profit?
Ford Mustang Fastback - USA. Mike tracks down a classic Ford Mustang Fastback in the US. Can Edd spin this iconic car into peak condition without blowing their budget?
Henry and Sam look around an incredible Americana collection in Cornwall. After examining an array of classic motors, Sam springs a big surprise on Henry.
Henry and Sam are invited to look around the workshops of the National Motorcycle Museum. The guys consider bidding on a 1926 Ariel and a 1927 BSA.
Find out how firemen's boots, garden tools and automated machines are made, as more objects are put under the microscope.
Find out how plastic bottles, eggs, jars and handcrafted wooden pens are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Steve is excited to work on a quirky train set that was built by a celebrated cartoonist and inventor. Plus, Amanda and Julie repair a much-loved doll.
Surprising Finds. Drew and Viki hunt down surprising and desirable items. They visit a classic car collection in Durham and a favourite dealer in Edinburgh.
Trusted Contacts. Drew hits the road with Viki and Alister to visit some of his most trusted contacts, including a favourite dealer in Kent and a new shop in Lancaster.
The Dust Devils face a nightmare journey to their remote mining lease. The Ferals are set a huge gold target, and the Poseidon Crew find a nugget.
A machinery problem adds more woes to the Dust Devils' nightmare start. Shane and Russell find gold, while the Poseidon Crew hunt for an intruder.
It's the final face-off for Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell as they search the shops of Lincolnshire for bargains. A hefty horticultural item and delicate posy holder go to auction.
Missing Jewels And Mysterious Artwork. Angus helps Amanda and Steve clear their late parents' home in Huddersfield that's full to brimming with memories and treasured things.
Steve is excited to work on a quirky train set that was built by a celebrated cartoonist and inventor. Plus, Amanda and Julie repair a much-loved doll.
Sam and Henry travel to Leicestershire to meet former motorbike racer Eddie Roberts. His impressive collection includes a rare 1962 Greeves Sportsman.
The guys are in Herefordshire to look at a eclectic collection. Henry sets his sights on a Nuffield tractor, while Sam wants an antique bird scarer.
Beach Buggy. In Leicestershire, Henry and Fuzz explore a shed full of classics, selecting a 1970s VW Beetle Beach Buggy. Can they restore it in time for a Somerset beach ride?
Angus is in Fife to dig through three generations of family clutter. They uncover a rocking horse and a collection of rare Beatrix Potter figurines.
Angus and the team search for interesting items at a historic home in Coventry. Later, they travel to Aberdeenshire to collect some unusual Asian furniture.
James Braxton and Raj Bisram continue their journey through the New Forest. They search for antiques before heading to auction in Shrewsbury.
James Braxton and Raj Bisram are on the last leg of their trip. They head to auction in Somerset - which one of them will make the biggest profit?
Tristan. In Chelmsford, Henry and Fuzz visit Fiat specialist Tristan, who has 500s, 600s, 126s and a Fiat minivan that once starred in an Indiana Jones movie.
Dominic. The boys are in Oxfordshire browsing through Dominic's restoration projects, including a Triumph TR4, a Jaguar XJS and a hotrod with a Rover engine.
Susan hopes restoration expert Steve can bring her once beautiful trinket back to life. Plus, Dominic revives a beloved wedding present, a Georgian peat bucket.
Leather expert Susie revives some damaged motorbike saddle bags. Stained-glass craftsman Matt and blacksmith Dom work on a decorative window panel with a remarkable story.
How do they power the next generation of race cars that run on electricity? Plus, how do they make mead - the favourite drink of the infamous Vikings?
How do they build the steam-powered paddleboats that cruise the Mississippi River? And, how did they create the defence system called 'the waterwall'?
How do they make modern paving stones and how has the process changed over thousands of years? Plus, how do they create a traditionally tailored tuxedo suit?
How do they fabricate fire gloves for emergency services personnel? Plus, how do they create the enamel that is used in bake-wear around the world?
Mercedes SLK. Mike tracks down a stunning Mercedes SLK. But can Edd make this classic car roadworthy without blowing their small budget?
Aston Martin DB7. Mike gets a great deal on a rundown Aston Martin DB7. Can Edd restore this classic at a profit - or will hidden problems blow their budget?
Sam and Henry travel to Leicestershire to meet former motorbike racer Eddie Roberts. His impressive collection includes a rare 1962 Greeves Sportsman.
The guys are in Herefordshire to look at a eclectic collection. Henry sets his sights on a Nuffield tractor, while Sam wants an antique bird scarer.
Cookies, loudspeakers; more everyday items are put under the microscope as we find out how they are made.
Wood burning stoves, ballet slippers and buses. More everyday items are put under the microscope as we find out how they are made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A Lawnmower Has More Horsepower! Wayne gets a lead on a 1956 Citroën 2CV in North Carolina. Possibly one of the first to be imported into the US, it's an offer he can't refuse.
Keels & Wheels. Wayne's recommissioning his 1934 Lincoln KB Convertible for the Keels & Wheels concours d'elegance in Texas. But will he get the price he's looking for?
F Is for Ferrari. Wayne drives to Maryland to inspect a 275 2cam Ferrari owned by the heirs of Parker Pens. Later, he checks out a Thunderbird convertible.
American All Stars. Wayne buys a 1941 Ford Woody with only 15,000 miles on it. He'll have Roger give it the once over and then plans to sell it at the Bonhams auction in Greenwich.
1965 Land Rover IIA. Edd and Mike set the challenge to find the ultimate bug-out vehicle that would ceaselessly run, and there was only one contender: the Land Rover Series IIA!
Land Rover Series 1. Mike finds a rare Land Rover Series 1 that may not be all it seems. A simple cosmetic touch-up becomes a complex and pricey restoration.
Rover P5 B. Mike has unearthed a quintessential piece of British motoring history with a Rover P5 B. The car appears to be in good nick, but Edd's upgrades won't come cheap.
Land Rover Discovery - Part 1. Auto ace Mike is on the hunt for a Land Rover Discovery. Having found one for £1,500, he whisks it back to mechanical marvel Edd to work his magic.
Land Rover Discovery - Part 2. Edd has been working hard on a Land Rover Discovery TDi. After a thrilling road test Mike has a buyer ready, but was the transformation worth it?
Land Rover Defender. Mike tries to find a V8-powered Land Rover Defender that Edd can turn into a cool urban cruiser. With just £5,000 can he find a decent car and spec it up?
Range Rover. Mike buys a bargain Range Rover P38 and Edd sets about mending it. They head to the wilds of Exmoor to give the car a final test drive before searching for a buyer.
Range Rover Mk3. Mike buys a clapped-out old Range Rover Mk3 for a bargain price of £5,000. His plan is to re-spray it, pass it on to Edd to fix, then double his money with the sale.
Mini Clubman. Henry and Fuzz find a Mini Clubman racing car in Surrey. Can they get this track legend back to winning ways, or will it stay at the back of the pack?
Triumph. Henry and Fuzz discover a shed full of rare gems in Bedfordshire. But a Triumph TR6 steals the show! Can they restore it to its former glory?
Standard Teignmouth. Henry and Fuzz find a rare 1929 Standard Teignmouth pick-up. Intrigued by this unusual beast, can they figure out what they're going to do with it?
Sporting Trials Car. Henry and Fuzz find a rare custom IRIS Sporting Trials Car. It's irresistible for the boys who get to work returning it to its former glory.

programmes.show_name: Francis Bourgeois & Chris Harris: We Saved A Train
programmes.show_description: Diesel In Distress. Trainspotting superstar Francis takes Chris to the world's biggest train show to recruit him for the epic restoration of diesel royalty.
Model T Ford. Henry and Fuzz arrive at Cheltenham and find Andy's vintage Model T Ford, which is in desperate need of restoration. Are they up to the task?
The team restores a historic Ukrainian painting. Plus, a 1960s bookings calendar from Liverpool's Cavern Club records an early Beatles performance.
Lost Oils And Hidden Porcelain. Irita finds old Italian paintings and the rarest porcelain she's ever sold, and an aspiring antiques dealer finds out if he has an eye for a bargain.
Brent and his son Cayden set off on an adventure to find highly valuable, crystalline gold. Plus, Sheryl and Simon hit it big on new ground.
